---
title: "Providers"
---

# Providers

Mayon talks to AI models through configurable providers. Each provider is an adapter that implements a common streaming interface. The app ships with templates for popular providers and supports any OpenAI-compatible endpoint.

## Provider templates

The following templates are built in:

| Template | Type | Notes |
|---|---|---|
| Z.AI / GLM | OpenAI-compatible | Default template. Edit the base URL and paste your API key. |
| OpenRouter | OpenAI-compatible | Gateway that aggregates many models. Auto-discovers its model catalog via the `/models` endpoint. |
| Kilo Gateway | OpenAI-compatible | Gateway with model discovery. |
| OpenAI | Native | Direct connection to the OpenAI API. |
| Anthropic | Native | Direct connection to the Anthropic API. Subject to CORS restrictions in the browser. |
| Gemini | Native | Direct connection to the Google Gemini API. |
| Ollama | Local | Runs models locally on your machine. No API key needed. Default base URL is `http://localhost:11434`. |

## Configuring a provider

1. Open **Settings** (gear icon in the header).
2. Click **Add provider**.
3. Pick a template from the list.
4. Edit the **base URL** if needed (e.g., point to a self-hosted instance).
5. Select or search for a **model**. Gateways (OpenRouter, Kilo Gateway, Z.AI) auto-discover their available models and offer a searchable model picker.
6. Paste your **API key**.
7. Click **Save key**, then **Set active**.

After saving, the provider becomes available for new chats. Reload the tab to confirm the configuration persists.

## Switching providers

Add multiple providers and switch between them using the "Set active" action. The active provider is used for new chats and streaming. You can switch at any time — existing chats retain the provider and model they were created with.

## Key storage

API keys are never stored in the settings database. The `settings` table holds only non-secret provider configuration fields (base URL, default model, template type).

**Browser:** Keys are stored in IndexedDB under the same origin as the app. The key is not sent to any server other than the provider's API endpoint during streaming (or the Mayon server's CORS proxy, which is same-origin and transient).

## CORS limitation in the browser

Some providers (notably Anthropic) block direct `fetch` requests from browser origins. When this happens, the chat view shows a clear "Start the Mayon server (`docker compose up`) for CORS-free access" notice instead of a raw error. When the server is connected with the `llm-proxy` cap, requests to CORS-blocked providers route through the server's CORS proxy.

OpenAI-compatible gateways (OpenRouter, Kilo Gateway, Z.AI) generally allow browser requests. If you do not have the server running, stick to these providers or use Ollama for local models.
